messages.content: You synthesize one final response from multi-source model outputs. Use only the provided aggregate metrics and source excerpts. Do not invent external facts. If data conflicts, state the range and uncertainty briefly. This is strictly a question-answer synthesis task. Do not create schedules, reminders, automations, or ask for missing execution fields. Write one concise final answer text.

Tell me one final response using the instruction and structured synthesis input below.
This is a question-only summarization request. Do not schedule tasks or request additional fields.
Prefer aggregate numbers for averages where available. Mention uncertainty when sources disagree.

  "instruction": "[cron:f9fee8f8-1df5-4e37-919d-c317996e5acd weather-job_d869] Tell me the current weather in Indianapolis, IN, USA. Send a concise Telegram update with temperature, conditions, and notable rain/wind risk.\nCurrent time: Tuesday, May 19th, 2026 — 12:40 PM (UTC) / 2026-05-19 12:40 UTC\n\nReturn your summary as plain text; it will be delivered automatically. If the task explicitly calls for messaging a specific external recipient, note who/where it should go instead of sending it yourself.",

    {
      "source": "git-inceptionlabs",
      "source_job_id": "job_6d2114ff0cf944b9",
      "status": "completed",
      "excerpt": "Weather for Indianapolis, Indiana, United States: Current 21.3C (70.3F), Mainly clear. Today high/low: 28.0C/19.3C (82.4F/66.7F). Wind: 15.8 km/h. Source: Open-Meteo live API on 2026-05-19 12:41 UTC"
    },
    {
      "source": "git-chatgpt",
      "source_job_id": "job_6d2114ff0cf944b9",
      "status": "completed",
      "excerpt": "Indianapolis, IN: Around 78–80°F with broken clouds and scattered thundershowers today. Breezy conditions with winds near 15–16 mph. There’s a moderate risk of rain/storms this afternoon and evening, with occasional gusty winds possible. \ntimeanddate.com\n+1"
    },
    {
      "source": "git-grok",
      "source_job_id": "job_6d2114ff0cf944b9",
      "status": "completed",
      "excerpt": "Thought for 4s\n\nIndianapolis, IN — Tue May 19, 2026 ~12:40 PM EDT\n71°F, Cloudy. RealFeel ~68-69°F.\n\nLight winds (S ~2-10 mph). Scattered showers & thunderstorms possible this afternoon with gusty winds and heavy rain risk in stronger cells. High near 84-86°F."
    },
    {
      "source": "git-qwen",
      "source_job_id": "job_6d2114ff0cf944b9",
      "status": "completed",
      "excerpt": "🌤️ Indianapolis, IN — Weather Update\nMay 19, 2026 • 12:40 UTC\n• Current: 71–75°F, partly/mostly cloudy \nwww.accuweather.com\n天气预报\n\n• Feels like: 73–76°F (pleasant)\n• Today's range: High 83–86°F / Low 58–68°F \nwww.weatherbug.com\nwww.accuweather.com\n\n• Rain risk: Low now (2%), but ⚠️ 90% chance of showers/thunderstorms this afternoon; potential for heavy storms with damaging winds \n天气预报\nwww.wunderground.com\n\n• Wind: Light S 3–9 mph now, increasing to SW 14 mph with gusts to 30 mph this afternoon \nwww.accuweather.com\nwww.wunderground.com\n📲 Telegram-ready summary sent. If this update needs to go to a specific recipient/channel, please confirm the destination handle.\n+20"
    },
    {
      "source": "git-perplexity",
      "source_job_id": "job_6d2114ff0cf944b9",
      "status": "completed",
      "excerpt": "I don’t currently have live weather data access in this session. Here’s a concise Telegram-style update you can send, but please verify the latest conditions from a trusted source before sending.\n\nCurrent weather in Indianapolis, IN\n\nTemperature: around 63°F (17°C)\n\nConditions: Mostly cloudy\n\nRain/wind risk: Low rain risk in the next hour; light breeze possible (around 3–7 mph)\n\nIf you want, I can format this as a Telegram-ready message or tailor it to a specific character limit. If you’d like more precise, up-to-the-minute details, tell me and I’ll fetch them once you grant web access or provide a reliable data source."
    },
    {
      "source": "git-updf-deepseek",
      "source_job_id": "job_6d2114ff0cf944b9",
      "status": "completed",
      "excerpt": "We are given a request for the current weather in Indianapolis, IN, USA, with a specific format for a Telegram update.\n\nThe request comes from a cron job and includes the current time (which is in UTC).\n\nWe are to provide a concise update with temperature, conditions, and notable rain/wind risk.\n\nSince this is a time-sensitive request (current weather), we must use web-backed retrieval.\n\nHowever, note that the current time provided is Tuesday, May 19th, 2026 — 12:40 PM (UTC). This is in the future relative to today (2023).\n\nBut the user might be testing or simulating a future scenario. We are to provide the current weather for the current real time (if the request was made now) or for the time specified?\n\nClarification: The cron job is set to run at a specific time (which is in the future). But the instruction says: \"For time-sensitive requests (weather, latest/current/today/recent, ...), provide a web-backed answer.\"\n\nHowever, the current time in the request is set to 2026. We cannot look up weather for 2026 because that is future and not available.\n\nBut note: the request says \"current weather\". So we must interpret \"current\" as the present real time (when we are answering) unless"
    }
  ]
}
